subject: Geography
title: Shanghai: China's Global City
description: A quick win: understand Shanghai: China's Global City.

hook.text: In 1990, Pudong was farmland. Today it's a skyline of glass towers housing 28 million people.

buildup.text: Shanghai sits where the Yangtze meets the sea—a perfect location for global trade.

discovery.text: Its port handles more cargo containers than any other port on Earth.

twist.text: The city is sinking—parts drop 10 mm per year as groundwater is pumped and buildings press down.

climax.text: Shanghai built the future on soft mud, and now the ground is slowly giving way.

punchline.text: The fastest-growing skyline sits on sinking ground.

quiz.question: What was Pudong before Shanghai's development boom?
